summary: Initial playthrough QA scaffold only; no full FPS route exists, but the first route should cover widened structural entries, splitter-post branches, weapon-footprint traversal, hold-fire aim, release cleanup, weapon-tray switching, and textured foundation readability.
observations: No playable FPS route, reward choice, or transition exists yet.
The foundation map now uses 5-tile structural entries for door-like breaks.
Continuing wall posts near entries now require 3 tiles per branch and 6 combined tiles.
Hold-fire now controls continuous firing, aim zoom, weapon centering, and reduced-sensitivity look.
Weapon switching remains through the bottom weapon tray.
The foundation now has floor, wall, and roof textures plus a full-level roof.
Gameplay-height screenshot QA confirmed the colored texture read for orange walls, green floor, and purple ceiling.
decisions: Playthrough QA must include combat, pause, death/recovery, objective, reward choice, and transition.
Player-facing breakage should be reported before polish.
risks: Smoke tests can pass while the actual combat and reward loop feels broken.
Door-like entries can still feel sticky if future props or enemies reduce usable width below the 5-tile structural-entry rule.
Splitter-post branches can feel sticky if future divider edits reduce either branch below 3 tiles.
Sustained hold-fire may reveal sticky movement or camera issues near entries that one-shot smoke cannot catch.
Early texture screenshots can look blank/white if captured before GPU texture upload; browser smoke should wait for renderer texture count.
